exact_eval_stack_gate_recommendations.recommendation: All selected postprocess, pose, mask, renderer, selection, and runtime payload bits must be inside archive.zip or fixed contest code.
Every proposed stack needs its own archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py CUDA auth eval before any score, rank, or kill claim.
Compare exact PoseNet and SegNet deltas against the planner's synergy/antagonism flags; split or reject stacks with component collapse.
Runtime simplification atoms can promote only with deterministic inflate closure and budget proof on contest-equivalent hardware.
Fast-chip traces are diagnostic; only identical bytes confirmed on T4/equivalent custody can promote.

full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.charged_bytes_source: pr65 archive bytes - pr67 archive bytes
full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.description: Use PR65-style charged residual/postprocess slots only where reference-relative component trace utility clears byte cost.
Treat QP1/qpose pose bytes as a trained-renderer contract atom; do not transplant onto untrained renderer geometry without exact eval.
Promote mask grammar, pair/class/tile, and boundary atoms through charged mask-stream edits with pose-sensitive gates.
Allocate renderer QZS3/FP4/grouped quantization bits by component response, not byte reduction alone.

full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.charged_bytes_source: observed PR67 archive bytes minus container bytes
full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.description: Use deterministic single-member layout, fixed names, and length-table packing only when strict ZIP custody remains valid.

full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.charged_bytes_source: runtime-only atom; byte value requires built archive
full_pipeline_opportunity_atoms.description: Prefer fixed-segment deterministic decode paths when they reduce inflate risk without hiding score-affecting side information.
Use RL/bandit/multipass search only as offline proposal policy; final selected atoms and selector payload bits must be charged in the archive.

atom_allocation_table.method: reference-relative first-order component excess minus charged byte rate cost and deterministic interaction-risk penalty
